title: Bredell briefed on Bitou water
description: PLETTENBERG BAY NEWS - The Western Cape MEC...
bodyText: [{"type":"paragraph","text":"PLETTENBERG BAY NEWS - The Western Cape MEC for local government, Anton Bredell, visited Plett today, Tuesday 20 January, to avail himself of the water situation in Plett and the greater Bitou.","html":"PLETTENBERG BAY NEWS - The Western Cape MEC for local government, Anton Bredell, visited Plett today, Tuesday 20 January, to avail himself of the water situation in Plett and the greater Bitou."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"Bredell visited the Plett desalination plant at the mouth of the Piesang River, where the plant’s operation was explained to him by plant manager Christo Lombaard."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The MEC was accompanied by various senior Bitou managers, including Acting Municipal Manager Chris Mapeyi.","html":"The MEC was accompanied by various senior Bitou managers, including Acting Municipal Manager Chris Mapeyi."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Bredell, according to his spokesperson Wouter Kriel, will later visit the Akkerkloof Dam in Knysna, along with Western Cape Premier Alan Winde.","html":"Bredell, according to his spokesperson Wouter Kriel, will later visit the Akkerkloof Dam in Knysna, along with Western Cape Premier Alan Winde."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’","html":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’"}]
